verdict_reasoning: SCARB2 is a type III transmembrane glycoprotein with primary lysosomal/endosomal localization. However, it has a well-documented plasma membrane pool that functions as a receptor for enterovirus A71 (EV-A71) and other enteroviruses, enabling viral entry from the extracellular space. This dual localization—dominant endosomal/lysosomal residence alongside a functionally engaged surface pool—qualifies as contextual surface accessibility.
